competencies: Formulate and apply constitutive material models
Analyze anisotropic elasticity and inelastic material behavior
Evaluate fracture mechanics and fatigue under complex loading
Apply non-local and peridynamic modeling approaches
Use AI-supported and data-driven methods for material modeling
description_en: Advanced study of mechanical material behavior, constitutive modeling, fracture and fatigue, including non-local and AI-supported data-driven approaches.

competencies: Apply the finite element method to strength and structural analysis
Select appropriate meshing methods and material models
Evaluate simulation results and assess solution accuracy
Model contact problems and perform model reduction
Conduct eigenvalue, harmonic and transient structural dynamics analyses
description_en: Applied finite element analysis for product strength and structural simulation, covering meshing, material models, contact and structural dynamics.

competencies: Apply systematic product development processes
Use systems engineering approaches across disciplines
Carry out methodical conceptual and detailed design
Manage interdisciplinary design projects in teams
Apply quality-oriented product development methods
description_en: Methodical engineering design covering the product development process, systems engineering, interdisciplinary information flows and team-based project work.

competencies: Systematically develop, calculate and design products
Apply methodical design from idea generation to final product
Generate and evaluate alternative solution concepts
Establish function structures and clarify design tasks
Collaborate in interdisciplinary engineering design teams
description_en: Systematic product development from idea to final design, reinforced by an interdisciplinary engineering design group project.

competencies: Analyze and synthesize work systems holistically
Plan and design ergonomic, human-centred workplaces
Apply assessment methods for work environment factors
Prepare and use planning-relevant data for factory planning
Optimize human-machine task allocation
description_en: Methods for planning and designing ergonomic work systems within factory planning, focusing on human-centred work conditions and human-machine allocation.

competencies: Plan and control industrial production processes
Derive organizational structures from corporate strategy
Apply ERP-supported production planning methods
Plan resource deployment and material provision
Evaluate production systems using logistic key figures
description_en: Planning and control of industrial production, covering organizational structures, order processing, ERP-based resource planning and production system evaluation.

competencies: Apply CAx tools across the product lifecycle
Build 3D CAD models using B-Rep, CSG and parametric methods
Use CAE simulation including FEM, CFD and multibody systems
Apply topology and shape optimization techniques
Integrate CAP and CAM in the digital process chain
description_en: Overview of CAx applications across the product lifecycle, covering CAD modeling, CAE simulation, optimization, and CAP/CAM process integration.

competencies: Use computer-aided tools in product development
Build product models and apply 3D modelling procedures
Familiarize quickly with CAx systems
Manage product data, interfaces and archiving
Apply Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) fundamentals
description_en: Foundational computer-aided design course covering CAx systems, product models, 3D modelling procedures, product data management and PLM basics.

competencies: Plan introduction and migration of CAx systems
Assess the economic efficiency of CAx systems
Apply cost methods to predict product costs across the lifecycle
Manage CAx systems and applications
Evaluate new technologies using the BAPM method
description_en: Management of CAx systems, covering system introduction and migration, economic efficiency assessment, lifecycle costing and technology evaluation.

competencies: Identify information and coordination deficiencies in supply networks
Apply approaches and rules for collaborative management
Conduct solution-oriented negotiations and contracts
Use collaborative IT tools to control supply networks
Design and apply key performance indicator systems
description_en: Collaborative management of supply networks, addressing coordination deficiencies, trust-based partnerships, negotiation, and collaborative IT control tools.

competencies: Identify information and coordination deficiencies in logistics networks
Apply rules of behavior for collaborative management
Conduct solution-oriented negotiations and contract talks
Use collaborative IT tools to control supply networks
Design key performance indicator systems
description_en: Collaborative management in logistics networks, covering coordination deficiencies, win-win partnerships, negotiation and collaborative IT control tools.

competencies: Apply tensor algebra to mechanical field problems
Describe kinematics of deformable bodies in space and time
Perform stress analysis of continua
Formulate balance equations for mechanical and thermodynamic problems
Apply principles of material theory
description_en: Introduction to continuum mechanics field theories, covering tensor algebra, deformation kinematics, stress analysis, balance equations and material theory.

competencies: Apply data-driven methods to dynamic mechatronic systems
Use supervised and unsupervised learning techniques
Build and apply perceptrons and neural networks
Apply data-driven control and reinforcement learning
Use physics-informed machine learning and assess its limitations
description_en: Introduction to data-driven and machine learning methods for the development, analysis and optimization of dynamic mechatronic systems.

competencies: Explain operating principles and limits of additive manufacturing techniques
Apply design rules and optimization strategies for AM
Specify powder requirements for different AM processes
Relate processing methods to AM build properties
Apply nondestructive inspection methods and AM standards
description_en: Additive manufacturing technologies covering process principles, design rules, powder requirements, process-property relationships, and nondestructive testing.

competencies: Create idealised discrete element method (DEM) models
Calibrate DEM contact model parameters
Run DEM simulations using LIGGGHTS software
Analyse and interpret DEM simulation results
Couple DEM with FEM, CFD and multibody dynamics
description_en: Discrete element method for simulating granular and particulate systems, covering modelling, calibration, LIGGGHTS simulation projects, and coupling with FEM/CFD.

competencies: Model and simulate dynamic multibody systems
Apply numerical methods to solve differential equations of motion
Analyse motion under acting forces in biomechanical contexts
Solve forward dynamics using time integration and muscle models
Solve inverse kinematics and dynamics from measured data
description_en: Modelling and simulation of multibody motion dynamics with a biomechanical focus on exoprostheses, including forward and inverse kinematics and dynamics.

competencies: Explain the structure and functions of electric drive systems
Select electrical drive systems for traction applications
Assess steady-state and dynamic drive characteristics
Analyse speed-torque behavior of electrical machines
Apply control strategies for variable-speed drives
description_en: Controlled and regulated electrical drive systems with a focus on traction drives, covering structure, machine characteristics and speed control strategies.

competencies: Select energy sources and storage technologies for applications
Assess fuels by availability, sustainability and economics
Explain electrochemical principles of primary and secondary cells
Evaluate kinetic and electrical energy storage systems
Apply battery management and scaling for mobile systems
description_en: Energy sources and storage technologies for vehicles, covering fossil and biogenic fuels, hydrogen, kinetic and electrochemical storage, and battery management.

competencies: Engineer and design complete technical systems
Apply engineering design approaches across disciplines
Evaluate requirements and boundary conditions for design tasks
Generate and assess alternative solutions
Lead and collaborate in interdisciplinary engineering teams
description_en: Engineering design of complete systems covering development, calculation and machine elements, reinforced by homework and a semester design project.

competencies: Implement engineering tool chains for production systems
Apply model-based systems engineering practices
Model engineering data using AutomationML
Design engineering tool interfaces using common concepts
Solve bilateral and multilateral data exchange problems

competencies: Analyse and model complex production systems from a systems engineering view

Perform trade-off analysis between competing system objectives

competencies: Apply operation management and ERP fundamentals
Perform demand forecasting and demand modeling
Plan inventory, capacity and aggregate production
Apply material requirements planning (MRP) and ERP

description_en: Introduction to operations management and enterprise resource planning, covering forecasting, inventory, MRP/ERP, capacity planning and network optimization.

competencies: Determine load assumptions for working and conveying machines
Calculate forces and moments for drive and component sizing
Derive load spectra for dimensioning machine drive units
Design and conceptualize complex machine assemblies
Apply multibody simulation to size complex machines
description_en: Design of working and conveying machines, focusing on deriving load assumptions and spectra and applying multibody simulation to size complex assemblies.

competencies: Select appropriate experimental measurement methods independently
Measure deformations, stresses and vibrations in solids
Apply strain gauges, photoelasticity and frequency analysis
Acquire and process measurement data
Conduct experiments and correctly evaluate results
description_en: Experimental mechanics linking theory and application through laboratory measurement of deformations, stresses and vibrations using strain gauges and photoelasticity.

competencies: Engineer and implement automated production processes

Program programmable logic controllers using IEC 61131-3
Program industrial robots

description_en: Factory automation and industrial robotics covering automated production engineering, PLC programming with IEC 61131-3, and industrial robot programming.

competencies: Explain the structure and function of driver assistance systems

description_en: Driver assistance systems and autonomous driving, covering vehicle dynamics, sensors and actuators, and stabilization- and navigation-level assistance functions.

competencies: Analyse types of vehicle emissions and their effects
Distinguish emissions from immissions
Apply legal regulations on vehicle emissions
Evaluate emission formation and reduction measures

description_en: Vehicle emissions covering emission types and effects, legal regulations, formation and reduction measures, and exhaust aftertreatment across powertrain concepts.

competencies: Analyse requirements in vehicle development
Design vehicle architectures and platforms

competencies: Model thin-walled structures using the finite element method

Perform deformation analysis of practical applications
Compute dynamic response in time and frequency domains
Solve eigenvalue and buckling problems

competencies: Apply design-for-manufacturing principles

description_en: Manufacturing- and assembly-appropriate product design, covering design-for-manufacturing and design-for-assembly principles, recycling and conformity assessment.

competencies: Design measurement setups from drawing specifications
Select appropriate measuring instruments and gauges
Apply measuring devices to inspect geometric elements
Assess measurement deviations and uncertainties

description_en: Manufacturing metrology covering measurement setup design, instrument selection, geometric inspection, measurement uncertainty and statistical data analysis.

competencies: Plan manufacturing steps for typical mechanical components
Select blanks and define technological datums
Schedule assembly and disassembly operations
Estimate required production resources
Apply production control, monitoring and quality planning
description_en: Manufacturing planning covering blank selection, process sequencing, assembly operations, resource estimation, production control and quality planning.
